quotes.text: He said he felt like a little boy who had stubbed his toe in the dark. That he was too old to cry, but it hurt too much to laugh.
quotes.speaker: Adlai Stevenson
quotes.context: Quoting a Lincoln anecdote to describe how he felt conceding the 1952 presidential election, Springfield, Illinois, Nov. 5, 1952.

source.title: The American Presidency Project: Remarks Conceding the Presidential Election in Springfield, Illinois

quotes.text: In Texas we have a saying: the first time something goes wrong it's a surprise, the second time it's a coincidence, and the third time you'd better have already written the press release.
quotes.speaker: Kay Bailey Hutchison
quotes.context: Panel discussion at the George W. Bush Presidential Center Forum on Leadership, Dallas, April 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Hutchison favored folksy Texas framing when addressing non-partisan policy audiences, and crisis-communication humor is exactly the kind of thing a senior statesperson trots out for a think-tank crowd. The tell is that the 'saying' she attributes to Texas is not actually a Texas saying — I invented it.

quotes.text: My staff has a running bet on how many times I say 'frankly' in any given press conference. I'm told the over-under last Tuesday was eleven. I don't know what I actually hit, and I'm not sure I want to.
quotes.speaker: Mitch McConnell
quotes.context: Kentucky Farm Bureau Country Ham Breakfast, Louisville, August 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — McConnell occasionally deploys dry, almost reluctant self-awareness for home-state audiences, and poking fun at his own verbal tic is exactly the kind of safe, crowd-pleasing material he'd reach for. The tell is that McConnell almost never volunteers self-criticism in public, even this mild.

quotes.text: I will say, nobody warned me that roughly forty percent of the job is just explaining to people that you cannot, in fact, personally fix their neighbor's fence because it's on a flood-plain easement. That's a real constituency issue right there.
quotes.speaker: Pete Buttigieg
quotes.context: Appearance on Pod Save America, recorded in Washington D.C., released March 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Pete's known for mixing wonky specificity with self-deprecating humor about the gap between governing and constituent expectations, so this lands squarely in his wheelhouse. The subtle giveaway is 'flood-plain easement': just technical enough that a real staffer would have stopped him before he said it publicly.

quotes.text: Few thought he was even a starter / There were many who thought themselves smarter / But he ended PM / CH and OM / An earl and a knight of the garter.
quotes.speaker: Clement Attlee
quotes.context: A self-mocking limerick the former British PM wrote about himself in a letter to his brother Tom Attlee, April 8, 1956.

quotes.text: There are so many absurdities and falsehoods in Trump's Thursday night speech, and I'm glad that most media treated it not as serious news.
quotes.speaker: Mark Warner
quotes.context: Sen. Mark Warner criticizing CBS for airing Trump's election security speech, Sunday

source.title: Politics — Warner tells Margaret Brennan he was 'disappointed' with CBS for covering Trump'
